name: HomeLight Real Estate Platform API
what_it_does: HomeLight Real Estate Platform REST API for agent matching, iBuyer/Simple Sale offers, and buy-before-you-sell financing. Enables AI agents to manage agent recommendation and matching for real estate consumer lead routing, handle Simple Sale instant offer generation for iBuyer lead conversion, access Buy Before You Sell bridge loan integration for contingency-free purchase workflow, retrieve agent performance data and transaction history for agent qualification, manage consumer lead intake and qualification for real estate referral network, handle transaction escrow coordination for digital real estate closing, access home valuation and market analysis for consumer pricing education, retrieve agent network management for referral program operations, manage lender and title partner integration for transaction coordination, and automate homebuyer and homeseller journey management for real estate platform workflow.
use_cases: Matching consumers with agents from real estate referral agents
Generating Simple Sale offers from iBuyer platform agents
Coordinating buy-before-you-sell from bridge loan agents
Qualifying agent performance from network management agents
not_for: Commercial real estate without residential consumer focus
Direct agent-to-consumer platforms without referral network model
best_when: A real estate lender, brokerage, or proptech company partnering with HomeLight wants AI agents to automate agent matching, instant offer integration, and consumer real estate journey management.
avoid_when: RESPA REFERRAL FEE RISK: HomeLight earns referral fees from real estate agents — automated agent referral arrangements must comply with RESPA Section 8; referral fee structures involving lenders, title companies, or other settlement service providers require careful RESPA structuring. Real estate license law — HomeLight's agent matching and referral model requires real estate license compliance in all operating states; state-specific referral fee and license law varies significantly. NAR settlement impact — post-2024 NAR settlement changes buyer agent compensation disclosure; automated buyer lead routing with undisclosed agent compensation arrangements creates NAR ethics and regulatory exposure.

auth.notes: HomeLight uses OAuth 2.0. API access via HomeLight partner program. Limited public documentation. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2012 by Drew Uher. Private (Zeev Ventures, Menlo Ventures, Group 11). $1.7B+ valuation (2021). Agent matching and Simple Sale iBuyer platform. Competes with Opendoor and Offerpad for real estate consumer platform. RESPA referral fee model creates regulatory sensitivity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No public free tier — partner program access
pricing.paid_tiers: Partner program; referral fee and transaction-based pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco, CA. Founded 2012. Zeev/Menlo/Group 11-backed. $1.7B+ valuation. Agent matching and Simple Sale platform. Competes with Opendoor for consumer real estate platform.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per partner API key; documented per integration

known_agent_gotchas: RESPA REFERRAL FEE RISK: HomeLight earns referral fees from agents it matches — lender or title company partnerships that benefit from HomeLight referrals must be structured to comply with RESPA Section 8 prohibition on kickback arrangements

State real estate license compliance — HomeLight's referral model requires real estate license compliance in each operating state; state referral fee laws and license requirements vary; automated referral activity must respect state-specific rules

NAR settlement disclosure — post-2024 NAR settlement buyer agent compensation disclosure requirements apply to HomeLight agent matches; automated buyer referral without compensation disclosure creates NAR ethics and regulatory exposure

Partner API access limitation — HomeLight API access requires direct partner program enrollment; very limited public API documentation makes capability assessment difficult without direct HomeLight engagement

Simple Sale offer volatility — HomeLight Simple Sale offers depend on iBuyer partner availability and market conditions; automated offer generation may return no offers or variable pricing in volatile markets
Consumer data privacy — HomeLight consumer real estate data is sensitive NPI; California CCPA applies to CA consumer data collected through HomeLight platform integrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Real estate consumer platform. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Consumer real estate and referral data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Real estate platform. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Consumer and agent referral data.
